mcqs.question: Which of the following best defines a software quality metric?

options.text: A qualitative description of software characteristics
A quantitative measure of the degree to which a software attribute meets requirements
A test case that verifies functionality
A user manual for quality assurance

mcqs.explanation: A metric provides a numerical measure of a specific attribute, allowing objective assessment of quality.

mcqs.question: In the ISO/IEC 25010 product quality model, which quality characteristic addresses the ease with which software can be modified?
mcqs.explanation: Maintainability is the characteristic that deals with the effort needed to make modifications to the software.

mcqs.question: Which metric measures the degree of interdependence between software modules?
mcqs.explanation: Coupling quantifies inter-module dependencies; low coupling indicates a better, more maintainable design.

mcqs.question: In object-oriented metrics, what does DIT represent?
mcqs.explanation: DIT measures the maximum inheritance depth from the root class to leaf classes, influencing system complexity.

mcqs.question: RFC in object-oriented metrics stands for:
mcqs.explanation: RFC counts the number of methods that can be executed in response to a message received by an object.

mcqs.question: A software component has 1200 lines of code and 30 defects discovered during testing. What is its defect density per KLOC?
mcqs.explanation: Defect density = number of defects / (LOC/1000) = 30 / 1.2 = 25 defects per KLOC.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a component used in the calculation of the Maintainability Index (MI)?
mcqs.explanation: MI is computed from Halstead Volume, Cyclomatic Complexity, and Lines of Code; defect density is a separate metric.

mcqs.question: A system has a Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) of 5 hours and a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of 100 hours. What is its availability (approximately)?
